meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve overwhelmed by AI tools (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome overwhelmed by AI tools. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: overwhelmed by AI tools

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Freelancer’s Guide to Speed
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in AI tools. Learn how to debug with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship projects faster and impress clients as a professional developer.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing every new AI tool. Focus on a high-leverage stack: Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent, CLI-based debugging. This combination automates the tedious triage process, allowing you to focus on high-value logic while maintaining production-grade code quality and meeting aggressive client deadlines.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. Three tabs are open: a stack overflow thread from 2018, a half-baked AI chatbot hallucinating syntax, and a client deadline looming like a thunderhead. You aren't just coding anymore; you're managing a chaotic ecosystem of fragmented AI tools that promise productivity but deliver mental fatigue. The overwhelm is real, and it is the silent killer of your freelance margins.

But what if you treated your workflow like a high-performance engine? By narrowing your focus to two surgical instruments—Antigravity and Claude Code—you stop 'using AI' and start 'orchestrating intelligence.' This isn't about more tools; it's about the right ones. Let’s strip away the noise and get back to shipping.

steps.title: Establish the Debugging Context
steps.description: Before running any commands, isolate the failure by providing the AI with the specific environment context. Clear context prevents hallucinations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and identifying the dependency graph. Summarize the likely root cause of the current runtime error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --context ./src --prompt 'Review the last three error logs and identify the dependency conflict.'

steps.title: Automated Bug Triage
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan the codebase for anti-patterns that correlate with your specific error message.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current file for common race conditions or memory leaks related to asynchronous calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --debug --task 'Identify and fix the promise handling issue in the auth controller.'

steps.title: Generate Surgical Fixes
steps.description: Instead of asking for a rewrite, request a targeted patch. This minimizes regression risks for your client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal patch for the identified bug. Ensure the fix follows existing style conventions.

steps.title: Verify with Unit Tests
steps.description: Never ship a fix without verifying it against the existing test suite. Use AI to generate the missing coverage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest unit test for the function I just patched to ensure the edge case is covered.

steps.title: Final Documentation and Cleanup
steps.description: Clean up the code and add documentation so the client doesn't call you back with questions tomorrow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add JSDoc comments to the modified functions and clean up any unused imports.

pro_tips: Always set a 'system role' in Claude Code that defines your specific coding standard to avoid generic, unoptimized code output.
Use Antigravity to map your project architecture before every sprint; it acts as a 'map' that prevents the AI from getting lost in deep nesting.
Pipe your build errors directly into Claude Code using 'npm run build 2>&1 | claude --stdin' for instant, context-aware debugging.
When facing complex bugs, ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the fix, not just the code, to build your own expertise.
Treat your AI interactions as a code review process; always review the generated diffs as if a junior developer sent them to you.
content.next_steps: Implement this two-tool workflow in your next client project, document your time saved, and begin standardizing your custom prompts into a reusable library.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with fragmented workflows and start building with professional-grade precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
